body.title: [sprint] Restore the intention edge: add `intentions` to LINK_KINDS / _LINK_KEYS
body.intention: I intend that a ticket or decision can point at the intention record it serves, so a build can be checked against the intention it claims to satisfy instead of against a prose sentence that dies with the ticket.
body.description:
On 2026-06-26 goals were retired in favour of intentions (T-skills-goals-to-intentions, closed shipped-unproven). That ticket removed `goals` from `LINK_KINDS` (memory_emit.py) and `_LINK_KEYS` (ticket_store.py) and reframed /sorted's 'Which goal' question to 'Which intention'. It never added the replacement edge. Today LINK_KINDS = (decisions, tickets, commits, whys) — there is NO `intentions` edge kind, so nothing can point at an intention record.

Measured consequence: the intentions store holds 2 records, while 444 tickets carry a free-text `intention:` prose string and 215 carry `intention: null`. Intentions were captured 444 times in a shape that cannot be pointed at, deduped, or proven. T-reorg-parents-path-sweep — the ticket the aider gate closed PASS on three 0-byte files — carried `intention: null`; the gate had nothing to check the build against.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py (LINK_KINDS), unseen_university/ticket_store.py (_LINK_KEYS), the schema-parity test that pins the two together.
**Design rules:** canonical memory home devlab/runtime/memory/; memory_emit.py is the single write chokepoint; JSON flat-file; NO SQLITE.
**Scope boundary:** IN — add `intentions` as an edge kind in both places, keep the schema-parity test green, prove a ticket emitted with links.intentions=["I-..."] round-trips and is grep-able by intention id. OUT — the intention envelope schema + validation + context-load surfacing (T-intentions-store); backfilling the 444 prose intentions (T-extract-intentions-from-chatlogs); /sorted intention-mapping (T-sorted-intention-open-then-map); the intent extractor (T-intent-extractor-agent).
**Test plan:** extend the existing schema-parity node (test_envelope_schema_matches_memory_emit) — it already proves the two key tuples must match; T-skills-goals-to-intentions' close note confirms re-adding a kind to only one side makes it fail. Red form: assert 'intentions' in LINK_KINDS fails at HEAD; green after. Plus a round-trip: emit a ticket with links.intentions, read it back, assert the edge survives.
**Completion criteria:** `intentions` present in both LINK_KINDS and _LINK_KEYS; schema-parity test green; round-trip test green; `grep -rl I-self-improving-process devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/` can find a ticket by its intention.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.completion_criteria: intentions in LINK_KINDS and _LINK_KEYS; schema-parity test green; links.intentions round-trips through ticket_store; a ticket is findable by intention id via grep.
